contentBlocks.title: Why convert SVG to PNG?
contentBlocks.html:
SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) is a vector format perfect for logos, icons, and diagrams because it scales without losing clarity. However, many platforms — social media, messaging apps, email clients — do not accept SVG files.

PNG is a universally supported raster format. Converting SVG to PNG produces a high-quality image that can be shared, embedded in documents, or uploaded anywhere.

This converter rasterizes SVG graphics at their native resolution using the browser’s rendering engine, producing clean, anti-aliased PNG output. Everything runs locally — no server uploads.

items.title: 2. Convert

items.title: 3. Download PNG
items.description: Download each file or grab them all at once. Size comparison shown for every file.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert SVG to PNG?

items.title: Social media
items.description: Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn do not accept SVG. Convert to PNG for clean, sharp uploads.

items.title: Email and messaging
items.description: Most email clients strip SVG attachments. PNG is displayed inline by every major client.

items.title: Documents and presentations
items.description: Word, PowerPoint, and Google Slides handle PNG natively. SVG support is inconsistent.

items.title: Print materials
items.description: Some print workflows require raster images. Convert SVG to PNG for pixel-perfect prints.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about SVG to PNG conversion

items.question: Does converting SVG to PNG lose quality?
items.answer: SVG is resolution-independent, so the rasterized PNG matches the SVG’s defined dimensions. The output is a high-quality, anti-aliased image.

items.question: Is transparency preserved?
items.answer: Yes. If the SVG has transparent areas, the PNG will retain alpha-channel transparency.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes. No file limit — add as many SVGs as you need.

items.question: Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.

items.question: Does this work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, but we recommend a desktop or laptop for larger files.
